(Moderate), Sudrk (Nice Sight) and Andha (Blind). They are
Quick, Fierce, Sharp, Tender, Stable and Unstable.
The next limb of the Almanac is Yoga. The meaning of this
word is union or sum. In other words it is nothing but the
sum-total ofthe longitudes of the two luminaries. There are 27
Yogas corresponding to the 27 constellations. Just as the longitude
of a star is I30-20',even so the value of a Yoga is the same number
of degrees and minutes. When the sum of the longitudes ofthe
Sun and the Moon is Zero, the first Yoga, Viskambha, starts,
and it is also the end of the 27th Yoga, Vaidhrti. Let us see it
in practice. On Friday the 13th July J9S4 Vaidhrti expires at
5-35 P.M. (according to Lahiri's Ephemeris). The longitudes of
